dialog.stepLabel: Zhezhi: "I didn't expect such a big crowd at the hearing......"

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I didn't expect such a big crowd at the hearing...

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Hmm... S-should I take the stand myself? What do you think, little kitty?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Ah, maybe we can do a little show of... um... paws. Raise your left paw if you think I should testify, or your right paw if...

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: W-wait! You raised your left paw before I even finished my sentence... No, that doesn't count. Let's try again.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Okay, if you raise your right paw, I'll... let someone else handle it.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Hmm... right paw, huh? Well... I did actually want to tell everyone the truth myself. Let's give this another shot.

items.speaker: Juyao
items.text: Ms. Zhezhi, the hearing is about to start. If you would, please join us.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Now? Y-yes, Madam Juyao! I... I'll be there in a minute!

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: {PlayerName}, oh, you're here! It's... it's really nice to see you! And... I appreciate you coming.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I just handed over all the evidence we gathered from Mingyan's studio and the Exile's camp to the Patrollers.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Now the Shanyun Art Exchange can't deny the fact they were producing and trading art forgeries.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: But... there is one more thing. The Patroller said we still need someone to testify in the hearing.

options.text: Do you want me to testify?
You can do this. I believe in you.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: You're really kind, but... um, I'll be fine to do this myself. You've already helped me so much, even just having you here is a huge relief.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Some things, I've got to face on my own.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: That said... I, uh, didn't... didn't expect there would be so many spectators coming to watch...

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: If I get nervous and mess up any details, could you please help to remind me?

options.text: No problem.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Thank you! Having you here helps, more than you know.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: It's about time. We should probably go in, huh?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Eh? B-but... I've... I've never spoken in front of so many people before...

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: H-how about... you do the talking, and, um, I'll add details... if, if I have to?

options.text: You know this case better than I do.
Actually, I kind of forgot the details...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: We wouldn't want my lack of artistic expertise to give the Shanyun Art Exchange a chance to turn the hearing against us.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: You're right... Then... I'll... I'll do it myself...

options.text: Relax, I'll be here if you need me.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Don't worry, I've got your back. Just remember, you're the only one who can lay out the truth—the whole truth— for everyone to see.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: ...You're right.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I was the one who chose to uncover the truth behind this case. I can't back down now, not when it matters most.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I'll give it my all, I promise. Let's go.

dialog.stepLabel: Juyao: "With both parties present, this hearing is now in session. L..."

items.speaker: Juyao
items.text: With both parties present, this hearing is now in session. Let us begin.

items.speaker: Juyao
items.text: First, a brief overview of the case before us.

items.speaker: Juyao
items.text: Ms. Zhezhi alleges that the guilty party, Mingyan, implicated in the Tacet Discord Assault, was coerced into committing art fraud under duress.

items.speaker: Juyao
items.text: Therefore, her mental state was adversely affected, which led to her committing actions contrary to her will.

items.speaker: Juyao
items.text: Today, based on new evidence provided by Ms. Zhezhi and the result of this hearing, we will determine whether to revisit Mingyan's charges and consider if the Shanyun Art Exchange is indeed guilty of selling illegal art forgeries.

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: What?! With all due respect, this doesn't match what we discussed before!

items.speaker: Juyao
items.text: Mr. Baici, I remind you that any evidence or arguments presented before and during the hearing are valid.

items.speaker: Juyao
items.text: If you would like to add further information, you are welcome to do so at any point.

items.speaker: Juyao
items.text: Now, without further ado, either party may begin their opening statement.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Madam Juyao, th-the situation is...

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: Madam Juyao, as the head of the Shanyun Art Exchange, I, Baici, must clear our name and seek justice here today.

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: There are two points I'd like to address. Firstly, the authenticity of our artworks remains unimpeachable. Secondly, Mingyan's provocative creations stem solely from her own greed and vanity. She alone should bear responsibility for the assault.

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: Throughout my tenure at the Art Exchange, our foremost principle has been that of integrity, a well known fact. And yet...

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: Integrity in business is not enough to stave off discord.

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: We are aware of certain painters, rejected by the Art Exchange, nursing grudges, and resorting to threats...

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: They bombarded us with spite and slander, causing a public uproar simply because they didn't get what they wanted.

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: Should the public be swayed by these slanderous insinuations, that paint us as wicked fraudsters, then I'm afraid justice will never be served!

items.speaker: Juyao
items.text: Please control yourself, Mr. Baici.

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: Forgive me, Madam, but if this injustice persists, I will have no choice but to sue them for harming the Shanyun Art Exchange's reputation. They have damaged the trust we built with our clients!

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: I demand they compensate us for our losses and issue a public apology!

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: Such disgraceful behavior will not diminish us! Our principles will not waver, and we will protect the Shanyun Art Exchange from any ill intent!

options.text: He's actually blaming the victim now...
He's trying to change the topic...

items.speaker: Yan
items.text: I've bought paintings from Shanyun Art Exchange before. They were good quality, and their service was decent. Hard to believe they'd be involved in something like this...

items.speaker: Woyun
items.text: The honest always get the short end of the stick... But who's Mingyan? A painter? Never heard of them...

items.speaker: Qianzhang
items.text: Tsk, must be some nobody trying to get attention using the Shanyun's name.

items.speaker: Fuqing
items.text: Why is the Art Exchange always in the middle of some controversy?

items.speaker: Juyao
items.text: Order! Order!

items.speaker: Juyao
items.text: You may continue your statement.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Madam, allow us to present our account of the events.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: This all really began three days ago.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I was entrusted to investigate a certain "Arcane Artist" in the Tiger's Maw.

dialog.stepLabel: Gupan: "Hey, Rover! Took you long enough to get here!..."

items.speaker: Gupan
items.text: Hey, Rover! Took you long enough to get here!

options.text: So you were the one who ordered this task.

items.speaker: Gupan
items.text: Things are getting awfully strange around the mine lately. Figured you're just the one to untangle this mess.

items.speaker: Gupan
items.text: With your knack for solving mysteries, you'll figure this out in no time!

options.text: What happened?

items.speaker: Gupan
items.text: I don't know if you've ever heard of it, but there's a special folktale in Jinzhou...

items.speaker: Gupan
items.text: Word has it, there's this mysterious Arcane Artist whose paintings come to life as they paint, as if each brushstroke has a life of its own.

items.speaker: Gupan
items.text: Once their canvas dries, whatever they've painted leaps right off the page. Mythical creatures, the whole shebang—all real as day!

items.speaker: Gupan
items.text: The thing is, anyone who might've seen these paintings? Well, they vanish into thin air...

options.text: What does the mine have to do with all of this?
Sounds like a Forte to me.

items.speaker: Gupan
items.text: I've never heard of such unique Forte.

items.speaker: Gupan
items.text: Lately, I've been receiving reports about eerie monsters wandering the mine at midnight. Some witnesses even ran into them more than once!

items.speaker: Gupan
items.text: These monsters, they look like paintings from a distance. But approach them and they're as docile as can be.

items.speaker: Gupan
items.text: Reading these reports, I couldn't shake the tale of the Arcane Artist from my mind.

items.speaker: Gupan
items.text: Sure, some say it's just a prank or some new Tacet Discord variant...

items.speaker: Gupan
items.text: But with rumors swirling, folks are getting jittery. Can't let things escalate. That's why I reached out to you.

options.text: Let me take it from here.
Good pay, good service.

items.speaker: Gupan
items.text: Great! Counting on you to crack this case wide open.

items.speaker: Gupan
items.text: Deal! I'll pay whatever it takes to keep the goosebumps coming. I can't wait for you to uncover the truth!

items.speaker: Gupan
items.text: Oh, and you might want to chat up the witnesses. Got their names right here for you.

dialog.stepLabel: Rover: "Nothing here. That's odd... Did I just imagine that?..."

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Nothing here. That's odd... Did I just imagine that?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: R-rover? You're here...

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Sorry, did I scare you just now?

options.text: I'm fine. Why are you here?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: ...F-finding inspiration.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: The night... often sparks my creativity...

options.text: ...Don't tell me you are actually the Arcane Artist.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Of course not!

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I... I heard about the tale of the Arcane Artist and hoped to find out who they are...

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Sorry I lied to you, I just... didn't want to drag anyone else into this...

options.text: You too? Why are you investigating this?
Are you also a ghost story enthusiast?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: N-no, I'm not into ghost stories.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Bringing painted creatures to life, making fantasies real—it sounds like a fairy tale, but when the Arcane Artist first caught people's attention in Jinzhou years ago, it was more than just a tale.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Now this story is making the rounds again. Most don't see the dangers it holds. As someone who feels the threat, I... I want to uncover the truth before it's too late.

options.text: The Arcane Artist showed up before?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Yes, there was once an artist who could create paintings that felt so real, you could almost step into them...

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: But these paintings, they strayed from the artist's original intentions and... caused a lot of trouble.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I don't know who's behind these latest events, but if it's really the Arcane Artist... their paintings will bring nothing but trouble. T-that's why I... I have to stop them, to prevent the past from repeating itself...

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Unfortunately, I'm... not very good at investigating, so I haven't found any major clues yet.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I'm here for the Arcane Artist, but also hoping to meet an old acquaintance. Maybe he knows what happened in the mine...

options.text: I'll go with you. It's linked to the case I'm investigating.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Is that so? O-ok.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: If this is the situation...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Hmm? {PlayerName}? Hey! {PlayerName}!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: What brings you here? It's been ages since we last met! The Patrol Station's been pretty busy lately, so I haven't had the time to catch up with you.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: And Zhezhi's here too! Hiya! Do you remember me? We met at the Moon-Chasing Festival.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Oh? Are you alright? You don't look well... Do you need help?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: H-hello... No, it's not... uh... no, thank you...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: The painting we had you do for us during the festival is awesome! Everyone loves it, so we put it up on the wall!

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Really? Hehe, I'm glad you all like it...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: We'd also like to invite you to our station, so we can formally thank you in person!

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Wait, how bout' coming to the station with me today? Are you heading back to the city?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Uh... but we're still investig— I... O-okay.

options.text: Are you sure we can go now?
Wait, we have a case to investigate.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Oh, is this not a good time?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Investigate? About what? A new case?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Oh, I get it! In that case, it's probably not the best time for a get-together.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Sorry...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: That's okay! Just tell me if you need any help!

options.text: Thanks. But why are you here, Chixia?

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: I'm here to support the Patrollers. There was an assault near the mine recently, and they're short on people to investigate.

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: What's odd is those monsters haven't caused any more trouble since we got here. Right now, we're talking to witnesses and trying to figure out what triggered the incident.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Did something just happen over there...

items.speaker: Chixia
items.text: Don't tell me it's a Tacet Discord... Hold on, just got a message... Seems like they really are stirring up trouble again. Sorry, but I gotta go check this out!

options.text: We'll go with you.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: S-should we go as well?

options.text: Why not? Maybe we'll find some clues useful to our case.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: You could be right, but there's one other thing...

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: After learning about the details of the assault, we decided to push forward with the investigation to get to the truth.

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: Pointless! The witnesses on the scene made it clear that it was all because of Mingyan's greedy ambition that she...

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I haven't finished my statement, Mr. Baici!

items.speaker: Cryptic Exile
items.text: Who are you?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Er, h-hello... we are...

options.text: We're here for Mingyan's painting. She's our friend.
We want to buy paintings.

items.speaker: Cryptic Exile
items.text: If it's her paintings you're after, why are you here?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: She... she wasn't in her studio, so we thought we could ask around.

items.speaker: Cryptic Exile
items.text: I see. Just wait for her here. When she leaves, it usually doesn't take long for her to return.

items.speaker: Cryptic Exile
items.text: You're here to buy paintings? That's not how this works. Go on, get out of here.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: W-we are actually here for Mingyan's paintings. She wasn't in her studio, so we thought we could ask around.

items.speaker: Cryptic Exile
items.text: Huh? Okay then, my bad.

options.text: What did you mean just now?

items.speaker: Cryptic Exile
items.text: ...It's nothing. Just wait for her here. When she leaves, it usually doesn't take long for her to return.

items.speaker: Cryptic Exile
items.text: Know her? We go way back. She's... okay in our books. Though she hasn't had the easiest run either, she helped us out a while back.

items.speaker: Cryptic Exile
items.text: Guess you could call us friends. She even gifted us a painting. It's somewhere around here...

items.speaker: Cryptic Exile
items.text: Ah, here it is! We're no fancy connoisseurs, but even we can tell that girl can paint!

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I kind of like it, myself. It's lively, bright... Almost like you can touch the freedom and joy she felt painting it.

options.text: Did something happen to her recently?

items.speaker: Cryptic Exile
items.text: Can't recall anything noteworthy...

items.speaker: Candid Exile
items.text: My guess? She's dabbling in some new, secretive art piece. I spotted her taking risky stabs at painting out in the wild.

items.speaker: Cryptic Exile
items.text: Well, now that you mention it, I do remember her talking about making art you could "step into" or whatever.

items.speaker: Cryptic Exile
items.text: But her little art excursions stirred up some unstable Frequencies. We warned her, but did she listen? Nope.

items.speaker: Chatty Exile
items.text: That's right! I saw her paintings myself! Tried to steer her clear, and what do I get? A lecture! Artists, always on their own wild wavelength, aren't they?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Sorry to interrupt, but mind if I ask what you normally do for a living?

items.speaker: Cryptic Exile
items.text: Us? Oh, the usual. A bit of hunting, some gigs here and there. Just the everyday grind.

options.text: Anything else?
You don't buy ore to make your own pigments?

items.speaker: Cryptic Exile
items.text: Nope, not many jobs around, just enough to scrape by.

items.speaker: Cryptic Exile
items.text: Uh... Oh! Right, haha, you reminded me. We do make and sell pigments occasionally.

items.speaker: Chatty Exile
items.text: Oh yeah, Mingyan buys from us often. We give her the lowest price, just as friends do!

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I have an idea.

options.text: Did you wanna say something just now?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Yes. I think... neither these Exiles nor Mingyan has told the truth.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I noticed some of the Exiles' hands were blackened... The thought suddenly hit me, what if it's from constant exposure to ink?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: If so, they must be making a lot of pigments and ink sticks...

options.text: But they lied to us and tried to cover it up.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: The painting they showed us and the artworks in the studio... they all match what Mingyan described in her diary.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Her diary shows us she had major mood swings, which really affected her art.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: All these strange things... started happening after the Shanyun Art Exchange got involved.

options.text: We can investigate the camp again tonight. What do you say?
Maybe there's more they're trying to hide.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Good idea.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Makes sense.
You two patiently wait until night falls.

items.speaker: Yinfeng
items.text: Who the hell are you?! Coming at us like this... Think you can just push around Exiles, huh?!

items.speaker: Candid "Exile"
items.text: It's them! They were here asking about Mingyan earlier today!

options.text: What were you saying just now?

items.speaker: Cryptic "Exile"
items.text: Who do you think you are?! We don't need to tell you a thing!

options.text: We just want the truth of Mingyan's case.
(Draw your sword)

items.speaker: Yinfeng
items.text: W-wait a second! Things don't need to get violent! We'll talk, okay?

items.speaker: Yinfeng
items.text: About Mingyan... Didn't my men answer your questions already? That's everything we've got on her, I swear. What more do you want?

options.text: Spill the truth or we won't let you go.
(Draw your sword)

items.speaker: Yinfeng
items.text: Alright, alright! I'll tell you everything!

items.speaker: Cryptic "Exile"
items.text: Yinfeng! We aren't supposed to...

items.speaker: Yinfeng
items.text: What? Can't you see that we're no match for {Male=him;Female=her}? Go ahead and pick a fight if you want, but I'm not risking my life for this.

items.speaker: Yinfeng
items.text: Now what else do you want to know? Just ask!

options.text: Did Mingyan get hurt because of you?
Why are you really making pigments and ink sticks?
Are you with the Shanyun Art Exchange?
I've nothing else to ask.

items.speaker: Candid "Exile"
items.text: If Yinfeng hadn't pushed her so hard, she wouldn't have gotten hurt. Now, she can't even pick up a paintbrush, much less finish this batch of paintings.

items.speaker: Yinfeng
items.text: Oh, quick to sell me out, huh? All I did was remind her to focus on her paintings.

items.speaker: Yinfeng
items.text: She's been rebellious and a bit too smart for her own good lately. I just wanted her to keep her head down and work. Who knew she'd crack and cause that mess.

items.speaker: Yinfeng
items.text: To cut our costs and keep our cooperation with the painters going. What else could it be?

items.speaker: Yinfeng
items.text: We... we're with the Shanyun Art Exchange. We're only pretending to be Exiles because our boss, Mr. Baici, wants this business kept quiet.

items.speaker: Candid "Exile"
items.text: You shouldn't have told them! Mr. Baici said no one can find out about the Art Exchange's involvement, no matter what!

items.speaker: Yinfeng
items.text: You just can't stop talking back, can you?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: So the Shanyun Art Exchange has been involved in Mingyan's case right from the start.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: No wonder they got all shifty when we brought up Mingyan earlier...

options.text: Masquerading as Exiles is indeed cunning.
Yinfeng... the name sounds familiar.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Yinfeng... Yes...

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: It was his signature on Mr. Shilang's transaction record.

options.text: We spoke right where it happened.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: When I asked him about the incident, he put all the blame on Mingyan, saying she had ulterior motives.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: The Exiles we questioned during the day did the same, trying to divert attention and cover up their own involvement at the scene in the first place.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: These distractions led us to concentrate solely on Mingyan, ignoring other possible angles.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I have a theory... but we need that "batch of paintings" they mentioned to test it...

options.text: Let's give the area another sweep.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: But there are so many more people in the camp now! How will we get in?

options.text: We'll walk right in.
We'll disguise ourselves as Exiles.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: They might hide all the evidence if we barge in like that...

options.text: Then we'll just have to "blend in."

items.speaker: Yinfeng
items.text: Huh? Why are you staring at me? Wait, I've told you everything I know! Aaargh—
With the Exile clothing in hand, the two of you quickly change into your disguises.

dialog.stepLabel: Baici: "That's quite the story. At best, this merely proves our invo..."

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: That's quite the story. At best, this merely proves our involvement in the incident at best, yet the real culprit remains unknown. Your accusations are groundless.

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: Allow me to remind you, she was the one who created those paintings, insisted on selling them, and concocted the entire terrifying incident.

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: Do you honestly believe her paintings would've earned a single penny without my intervention?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: No... Mr. Baici, you're changing the subject.

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: How ungrateful. She ensnared us in this debacle, and now you dare to lay the blame at our feet?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: B-Before we proceed, I wish to present this audio recording to the court.

items.speaker: Recording Playback
items.text: "Ugh, Mr. Baici stopped by again, grumbling about the delay in getting Mingyan's paintings to the Shanyun Art Exchange..."

items.speaker: Recording Playback
items.text: "If Yinfeng hadn't pushed her, she wouldn't have gotten hurt. Now she can't even paint, and our order is gonna be late."

items.speaker: Recording Playback
items.text: "She's been rebellious and a bit too smart for her own good lately. I just wanted her to keep her head down and work. Who knew she'd crack and cause that mess."

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Mingyan was initially only making copies. Everything changed after you stepped in.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Trickery, misinformation, and later on even threats... She was led down the path of forging artworks for you.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I do understand that... if her own heart hadn't wavered, none of this would've happened... But there was already no turning back by the time she wanted to quit.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: So, saying she was the mastermind behind it all... that's just not fair.

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: Even so, how is forgery a valid accusation? Nothing like that was mentioned in your recording, now was it?

options.text: We found the original painting in the hidden warehouse.
We found evidence of forgeries being bought and sold.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: This evidence clearly shows that these paintings are no ordinary copies, but your way of making a profit.

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: Heh, there's no such thing as real or fake paintings. You're not fooling anyone.

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: For it to be forgery, there has to be another original artist. These paintings are all the work of Mingyan. Even if she made dozens of identical copies, it wouldn't be considered forgery.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: ...Mr. Baici, do you recognize this painting?

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: Well, of course. It's "Serenity of Xiehua Village," the work of the Arcane Artist.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Then can you tell the difference between this one and the others?

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: Of course, this piece possesses a lifelike quality unmatched by any other, all thanks to the Resonance ability of the Arcane Artist—Mingyan.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: However, judging from these paintings, it seems Mingyan's ability never stabilized successfully, except for this original piece.

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: What does this have to do with anything? Do you have solid evidence to prove the differences between these paintings?

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: What you're saying is all speculation. There's no guarantee a painter won't make mistakes, is there?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Artists seeking perfection always choose the highest quality pigments for their most important pieces.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: During our investigation of the studio, I accidentally got paint on me and discovered something interesting about the materials used in these paintings.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: The original paintings had pigments that were purer and brighter, able to blend more naturally and retain color for longer.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: In contrast, the forgeries used pigments made from mineral scraps. These don't hold up the same, smudging at the slightest touch.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: We can distinguish the real paintings from the fakes simply by analyzing the ink used.

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: Doing so only proves the so-called "original painting" is created differently from the others. Are you implying that Mingyan is only allowed to use a single kind of pigment throughout her work?

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: How is this supposed to prove that we are behind the forgeries?!

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Because the original creator of "Serenity in Xiehua Village" is me.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I hear people talking.

options.text: We can eavesdrop on their conversation.
[Enter combat] Let's beat the truth out of them.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: OK, I'll use my Terminal to record it, just in case.

dialog.stepLabel: Baici: "Well, that was certainly a touching story. But I'm afraid yo..."

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: Well, that was certainly a touching story. But I'm afraid your words alone won't be enough to prove you created this painting.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: ...Madam Juyao, to prove my statement, I request your permission to use and paint with one of these copies.

items.speaker: Juyao
items.text: Granted.

options.text: Are you sure you want to do this?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Yes. Mingyan's experience has made me realize something.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: To create art that truly resonates, I must first accept myself.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: The paintings I drew in the past... they were heartfelt creations, pieces of my life. They formed my past and shaped who I am today.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: It's not just about proving a single painting... it's about so much more...

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: If my Forte is the cause behind all this, then I must take responsibility... I can't just let this go on and hurt others.

dialog.stepLabel: Zhezhi: "When I learned the truth, I was heartbroken......"

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: When I learned the truth, I was heartbroken...

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I believed that by sealing my Forte, no one would get hurt because of my paintings. I never expected them to continue causing harm after all these years.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: But {PlayerName}'s words made me realize something.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: It was never about the paintings themselves, but rather those who sought to use them to hurt others.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Mr. Baici, you run a highly esteemed calligraphy and painting institution, yet you've suppressed the creative spirit and violated these artists' dignity, all for the sake of wealth.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Everything they cherished has now become a tool for profit... The works they held dear are just tools for your gain, insulting to both the original artists and their imitators.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Set before you is the original painting from many years ago and the one I've just completed. Now, everything has been brought to light.

items.speaker: Juyao
items.text: We received word from the Public Security Bureau ten minutes ago that they have taken the Exiles into custody and found the location where the paintings were hidden.

items.speaker: Juyao
items.text: Mr. Baici, do you have anything else to add to your defense?

items.speaker: Juyao
items.text: Very well. We will re-evaluate Mingyan's charges, determine the Shanyun Art Exchange's involvement in the incident, and conduct a comprehensive investigation as soon as possible.

items.speaker: Juyao
items.text: This hearing is adjourned.

dialog.stepLabel: Zhezhi: "I think it's safe to say she'll give it some thought...."

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I think it's safe to say she'll give it some thought.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: During the few times we met, she was always passionate about discussing art with me. I think that fire inside her won't just fade away.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Come to think of it, the deep discussions we had and the painting duels back then felt like true friendship.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: So... seeing her in so much pain, I just had to help.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I don't want Mingyan to lose hope and have someone who loves painting so much just disappear...

options.text: I think she understood what you meant earlier.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: To be honest, I didn't just see this case through to correct mistakes or help the artists. Deep down, I...I didn't want to let you down.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I wanted to tell you this back at the mine, but the words just didn't come out right...

options.text: It's fine, I understand what you're trying to say.
I'm glad you finally said it.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Thank you for being my friend and helping me through all of this, {PlayerName}.

options.text: I can say the same to you.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: While I have yet to uncover my past and who I am, every moment with you is a new and special memory.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Thank you for your help, Zhezhi. This experience has helped me understand you and the world a little better.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: You seem to be pretty slow on the news, {PlayerName}.

options.text: So you know how to tell jokes now?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Hehe... You've given me the courage to joke around.

options.text: Does that mean you can do the same in front of others?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Hmm... well...

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Let's take it slow for now.

items.speaker: Mingyan
items.text: Zhezhi... It's been a while.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Your injuries...

items.speaker: Mingyan
items.text: The researchers have taken good care of me. A chat won't hurt. And you must be... the Rover?

options.text: You know me?

items.speaker: Mingyan
items.text: I heard about everything you did. Thank you for freeing me from those nightmares.

items.speaker: Mingyan
items.text: You know what, Zhezhi? You may seem soft and gentle on the outside, but if your boundaries are crossed, I think you can be tougher than anyone else.

items.speaker: Mingyan
items.text: And... I still owe you an apology.

items.speaker: Mingyan
items.text: Forgive me. Because I was so obsessed with wanting to be you... to be better than you, I've caused you so much trouble...

items.speaker: Mingyan
items.text: ...The pain of these injuries managed to put a stop to my obsession with imitating your paintings.

items.speaker: Mingyan
items.text: Heh. Maybe... I was never meant to be a painter to begin with...

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I brought you a gift...
Mingyan accepts a scroll of painting from Zhezhi, staring at it for a long time after opening it.

items.speaker: Mingyan
items.text: Hehe, see...? Only you can create a painting with such life. I still have a long way to go.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: This is your painting.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: It's the one you gave me when we first met.

items.speaker: Mingyan
items.text: I... Hehe... How ironic. I've been trying to imitate you for so many years that now I can't even tell my own paintings apart anymore.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Your paintings were never inferior to mine. Just look at this piece, even you approve of its value. It doesn't matter who painted it.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Mingyan, I hope you can find your true self, whoever she is. I know you can do it.

items.speaker: Mingyan
items.text: ...Thank you for bringing me this painting... Really.
Mingyan opens the scroll painting once again and stares at every detail for a long time. She appears lost in thought. Zhezhi and you leave quietly after seeing this.

dialog.stepLabel: Zhezhi: "So this Shanyun Art Exchange has been involved in Mingyan's ..."

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: So this Shanyun Art Exchange has been involved in Mingyan's case right from the start.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: No wonder they got all shifty when we brought up Mingyan earlier...

options.text: Masquerading as Exiles is indeed cunning.
Yinfeng... The name sounds familiar.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Yinfeng... Yes...

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: It was his signature on Mr. Shilang's transaction record.

options.text: We spoke right where it happened.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: When I asked him about the incident, he put all the blame on Mingyan, saying she had ulterior motives.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: The Exiles we questioned during the day did the same, trying to divert attention and cover up their own involvement at the scene in the first place.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: These distractions led us to concentrate solely on Mingyan, ignoring other possible angles.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I have a theory... but we need that "batch of paintings" they mentioned to test it...

options.text: Let's give the area another sweep.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: But there are so many more people in the camp now! How should we get in?

options.text: We'll walk right in.
We'll disguise ourselves as Exiles.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: They might hide all the evidence if we barge in like that...

options.text: Then we'll just have to "blend in."

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Okay.
With the Exile clothing in hand, the two of you quickly change into your disguises.

dialog.stepLabel: Unknown (83): "Some ore residues are scattered beside the trolley, with col..."

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Some ore residues are scattered beside the trolley, with color stains on it.

options.text: Looks like pigments...

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: The trolley is worn... Maybe it carried something very heavy.

options.text: Maybe the Exiles used it to transport ore and pigments.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Could it be they transported ore, made pigments from them, and then moved the pigments elsewhere?

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Could the buyers Mr. Shilang mentioned be the ones who stole the ore?

options.text: Let's see if we can find the evidence.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: After {PlayerName} and I bumped into each other, we decided to investigate the surroundings and follow Patroller Chixia to the scene of the incident.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: It was there we learned about the details of Mingyan's offense and the truth behind the tale of the Arcane Artist.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Once Zhezhi has the paintbrush in her hand, she begins to speak calmly.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (She'll be fine making the statement on her own now.)

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: After learning about the details of the assault, we decided to push forward with the investigation to get to the truth.

items.speaker: Baici
items.text: Pointless! The witnesses on the scene made it clear that it was all because of Mingyan's greedy ambition that she...

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I haven't finished my statement, Mr. Baici!

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: After {PlayerName} and I bumped into each other, we decided to investigate the surroundings and follow Patroller Chixia to the scene of the incident.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: It was there we learned about the details of Mingyan's offense and the truth behind the tale of the Arcane Artist.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Once Zhezhi has the paintbrush in her hand, she begins to speak calmly.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (She'll be fine making the statement on her own now.)

dialog.stepLabel: Unknown (83): "Zhezhi seems to be struggling to tell what she couldn't expr..."

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Zhezhi seems to be struggling to tell what she couldn't express earlier, but eventually stays silent.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Phew... Sorry to trouble you, {PlayerName}.

options.text: Why are you apologizing all of a sudden?
Don't worry about it.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Thanks... Uh, wait, I haven't said why.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: What I'm trying to say is...

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: If I had been clearer, you wouldn't have had to explain things to Chixia for me.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: She's really nice. It's just... we're not that close, and I'm not sure how to respond to how friendly she is... I didn't want to be rude, so I... I couldn't bring myself to turn her down.

options.text: It's okay. I got you.
Her friendliness is infectious.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: I'll do my best not to trouble you and express my feelings properly next time...

options.text: I'm sure you will.
No trouble at all.

items.speaker: Zhezhi
items.text: Thank you...

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Then both of you decide to ask people around about the situation.
